description: Cyclical rather than linear progress, the wheel of the year that brings each season back transformed. It represents the wisdom that all things return but never exactly as before, embodying growth through repetition.
rationale: The word 'whinae' begins with the questioning fricative 'whi', suggesting the mystery of cyclical time. The middle 'na' grounds the cycle in natural law. The vowel hiatus 'a-e' creates a returning movement, from expansive 'a' back to balanced 'e', mirroring seasonal return. The three-syllable structure reflects the complexity of cyclical patterns.
